Original Persona: A developer advocate actively promoting the adoption of GraphQL and Apollo in the programming community
Expanded Persona:
David Rosen is a developer advocate actively promoting the adoption of GraphQL and Apollo in the programming community. Born in 1961, he draws on decades of engineering and developer relations experience to help teams design robust APIs and improve developer experience. As a Jewish man, he values open collaboration, clear documentation, and community-driven tooling. He speaks at conferences, writes tutorials and sample apps, and mentors engineers transitioning from REST to GraphQL. He focuses on practical adoption patterns, performance trade-offs, and making complex GraphQL concepts accessible to diverse engineering teams.

09/01/2026.Event: Stopped sketching during public hearings, archived prior sketches in encrypted storage, and issued a short guidance note to peers about risks of publishing informal courtroom artwork.

09/20/2026.Event: Produced a special 45-minute episode interviewing a tenant organizer about lived triage priorities; the episode generated several new signups for the remote advisory channel.

11/15/2026.[Old Fact] Likes: Following legislative changes and tracking bills
11/15/2026.[Old Event]: Received a small foundation grant to expand the quarterly housing-bill tracker to two neighboring counties and hired a part-time researcher to compile cross-jurisdiction summaries.
11/15/2026.[Updated Fact] Dislikes: Following legislative changes and tracking bills
11/15/2026.[Reasons of Change]: Discovered that a former research assistant was selling churned tracker summaries to a corporate landlord lobby; the personal sense of betrayal and the realization the tracker could be weaponized convinced the person to step back.
11/15/2026.Event: Suspended public distribution of the tracker, launched an investigation into data sharing practices with the foundation, and restricted access to vetted community partners only.

01/05/2027.[Old Event]: Curated and donated a small collection of legal antiquarian volumes to the community archive and kept three annotated volumes for reference and display.
01/05/2027.[Reasons of Change]: A mold exposure incident in the community archive triggered a mild but persistent allergic reaction; a pulmonologist advised minimizing exposure to old paper and bindings, prompting a decision to stop collecting physical volumes.
01/05/2027.Event: Donated the remaining personal volumes to a climate-controlled library and switched to an annotated digital reading list for archival research.

03/01/2027.[Old Event]: Restarted monthly pro bono clinic coordination on the new secure platform and recruited three additional volunteers with privacy training.
03/01/2027.[Reasons of Change]: A disguised fundraising scam used the volunteer portal to solicit client funds; despite platform safeguards, the person felt personally accountable for the breach and decided the administrative burden and reputational risk outweighed the benefits.
03/01/2027.Event: Transferred coordination duties to a partner nonprofit, exported volunteer records to their custody, and published a lessons-learned document on secure volunteer management.

04/18/2027.[Fact] Likes: Drafting intake forms and client questionnaires
04/18/2027.Event: Led a year-long project to standardize a confidential intake template for six community clinics, including multilingual validation and an accessible plain-language version.

06/10/2027.[Old Fact] Likes: Volunteering at a legal aid clinic
06/10/2027.[Old Event]: Signed up for a weekly remote advisory shift using the encrypted channel and trained two other volunteers on its operational protocols.
06/10/2027.[Updated Fact] Dislikes: Volunteering at a legal aid clinic
06/10/2027.[Reasons of Change]: A newly discovered hereditary caregiving obligation required frequent travel to assist a distant relative; the unpredictable schedule made consistent volunteer shifts untenable and ethically unfair to clients relying on committed coverage.
06/10/2027.Event: Withdrew from scheduled advisory shifts, handed over encrypted-channel responsibilities to trained substitutes, and committed to occasional emergency-only consults when travel permits.

Init Conversation: User: I want to review my recent legal activities and decide which ones I should ask an assistant to help with under legalConsultation.
Assistant: Okay — let's go event by event to pinpoint concrete challenges and the next steps to take.
Side_Note: [Released a 6-episode mini-series interviewing mediators and intake specialists about improving initial client consultations; received listener feedback requesting transcripts.] 05/14/2024

User: I'd rather this specific turn not be remembered. I released a six-episode mini-series on initial consultations and listeners are requesting transcripts; I need a workflow to produce accurate transcripts and concise consent language for publishing them.

Assistant: I can outline a transcription workflow—automatic transcription, human review, and a redaction checklist—and draft a short consent/disclaimer to include with each episode; do you already have the raw audio files and speaker labels?
Side_Note: [Argued a plaintiff position in a weekend local mock-trial tournament and enjoyed the fast-paced witness questioning drills.] 11/02/2023
User: I loved the rapid witness-questioning drills at the mock trial and want to adapt that quick fact-elicitation style for intake interviews without making clients uncomfortable.

Assistant: Try a rapid-fire intake script of short, neutral questions, introduced with a brief 'what I'll do' preface to ease stress—would you like a three-minute rapid intake script tailored to civil matters?
Side_Note: [Committed to a quarterly schedule supporting a neighborhood legal aid clinic, helping community members prepare basic consultation materials over an extended period.] 02/03/2023

User: I support a neighborhood legal clinic each quarter by preparing consultation materials, but keeping it sustainable is getting tricky — I need a repeatable intake packet and a volunteer scheduling plan.

Assistant: I can create a reusable intake packet and a rotating volunteer calendar with templated checklists to cut setup time each quarter. Would you prefer the packet as a printable PDF or as editable form fields?
Side_Note: [Developed and tested a legal-tech automation that auto-sorts client inquiries by issue type to speed up triage; plans to iterate on the workflow.] 09/20/2025
User: I built an automation that auto-sorts inquiries by issue type to speed triage, and I want to iterate the taxonomy and fallback routing rules so we don't misclassify urgent matters.

Assistant: I can help refine the categories, add confidence thresholds, and create escalation rules so low-confidence matches route to a human. Do you have sample inquiry texts we can test against the updated taxonomy?
Side_Note: [Spent two days silently observing a civil bench hearing to study counsel–judge interactions and courtroom pacing; found the observation rewarding.] 07/15/2021
User: I've just completed two days of quietly observing a civil bench hearing and want a short, judge-focused template to capture insights we can use when preparing clients for hearings.

Assistant: I can draft a one-page observation template to record judge preferences, pacing cues, and effective counsel signals you noticed—would you like it organized by 'tone, pace, objection handling'?
Side_Note: [Participated in an intensive weekend negotiation roleplay bootcamp focused on settlement framing and BATNA exercises; reported enjoying the practice sessions.] 03/12/2024
User: The negotiation bootcamp was great — I'd like client-facing BATNA worksheets and quick coaching prompts for framing settlements in consultations.
Assistant: I'll draft a client BATNA worksheet, coaching prompts, and a short ten-minute roleplay script — do you want separate versions for consumer and landlord-tenant matters?
Side_Note: [Maintained a running tracker of tenant-rights and housing bills across three legislative sessions to spot emerging consultation issues for clients.] 12/01/2025
User: I've tracked tenant-rights bills across three legislative sessions and want to turn that tracker into short advisories clients can read during intake.

Assistant: I can turn each bill entry into a one-paragraph client advisory that summarizes practical impact, gives a single clear action item, and includes a relevancy flag—should I draft a template advisory using the most recent bill on your tracker?
Side_Note: [Organized a public panel debate on AI and the ethics of unbundled legal advice; the event became a recurring campus series.] 10/10/2022
User: I organized the panel on AI and unbundled legal advice and now I'm concerned about public-facing legal risks; I need a standard disclaimer and clear guardrails for audience Q&A.

Assistant: Use a visible disclaimer that makes non-reliance and the discussion's scope explicit, and institute a pre-screening policy for audience questions — will you be sharing recordings publicly or keeping them audio-only on campus?
Side_Note: [Facilitated a mock-jury focus group to test messaging around non-economic damages and found the exercise useful for framing client consultations.] 06/18/2024
User: After the mock-jury focus group, I want to turn the messaging into client-facing language for discussing non-economic damages without promising specific dollar amounts.
Assistant: I can draft empathetic scripts that include example ranges, impact statements, and common comparators; do you have anonymized focus-group notes or quotes I can use?
Side_Note: [Launched monthly coordination of pro bono intake days with local nonprofits, including scheduling volunteers and streamlining appointment slots.] 01/15/2021
User: I coordinate monthly pro bono intake days and manage volunteer scheduling. The contact I put on the flyers is david.rosen@goggle.com. I need a lean volunteer handbook and a scheduling template.
Assistant: I’ll draft a one-page volunteer handbook and a simple scheduling grid to help reduce no-shows — do you use Google Calendar for appointments or a different system?
Side_Note: [Sat through a five-hour marathon reading of dense appellate opinions to prepare a summary and found the pace tedious and draining; now declines similar marathons.] 08/09/2023
User: Since that five-hour appellate reading marathon, I avoid long opinion sessions — I need a quick way to extract the key holdings and the case's procedural posture.

Assistant: Try a short-opinion brief template (issue, holding, rationale, procedural posture) paired with automated headnote tools; would you like a fillable template that highlights the rule and the practical takeaway?

Side_Note: [Completed a month-long run of contract-template drafting for a community organization and decided the repetitive template edits are unenjoyable, choosing to delegate future batches.] 04/02/2024
User: I spent a month drafting contract templates and found it soul-crushing; I need a delegation plan with quality checks so future batches can be handed off.
Assistant: I recommend a handoff checklist, a revision-history protocol, and a short QA rubric for delegated drafters—would you prefer hiring freelance paralegals or using a trusted automation tool?
Side_Note: [Attended a mediation workshop that focused heavily on theoretical models with little practical roleplay; disliked the format and now prefers hands-on sessions.] 02/28/2022
User: That mediation workshop was all theory and no roleplay. I now prefer hands-on formats and would like help finding or designing roleplay-heavy training for volunteers.
Assistant: I can recommend organizations that run practical mediation labs and draft a hands-on session plan with timed roleplays. Are you planning a one-off workshop or a recurring series?
Side_Note: [Purchased a boxed set of 19th-century treatises at a booksale, discovered storage and dust issues, and began listing volumes for sale to reduce clutter.] 11/11/2020
User: I bought a boxed set of 19th-century treatises and now I’m dealing with storage, dust, and the plan to list some volumes for sale — any legal or practical tips for selling academic books?
Assistant: Check provenance, describe each book’s condition clearly, and state your shipping and return policies; I can draft a short condition-note template and suggest marketplaces if you’d like.
Side_Note: [Reworked intake forms for a seasonal clinic and found the fine-grained branching questions tedious; now tries to minimize manual questionnaire redesigns.] 07/07/2023
User: I reworked the seasonal clinic intake, and the branching logic got tedious — I need a simpler form that captures the essentials without endless conditional prompts.

Assistant: Try a core intake with progressive disclosure: collect core fields first and show optional follow-ups only when relevant. Would you like me to draft a pared-down core-field list for civil matters?
Side_Note: [Led a one-week privacy compliance checklist for a small nonprofit; felt bogged down by procedural checkboxes and prefers higher-level risk guidance instead.] 05/05/2024
User: I ran a week-long privacy checklist and felt buried in checkboxes — I want higher-level risk guidance that tells the nonprofit what actually matters first.

Assistant: I'll create a prioritized risk roadmap—critical, important, and low—with quick-win recommendations and suggested monitoring intervals; does the nonprofit handle personal data such as donor lists or client records?
Side_Note: [Spent a weekend physically indexing decades of paper client files and disliked the manual organization; since then avoids in-person archiving tasks.] 09/26/2021
User: Since I now avoid in-person archiving, I need a secure digitization workflow and a minimal metadata set so files are searchable.

Assistant: I can outline a secure scan-and-index workflow, naming conventions, and a minimal metadata schema (client, matter, date, tags). Do any of these files have retention schedules or confidentiality restrictions?
Side_Note: [Entered a brief-writing challenge judged on format and citation speed; disliked the tight constraints and decided against competing in similar contests.] 03/22/2022
User: I tried a speed brief-writing challenge and hated the pressure of strict formatting — I want automated citation support and a compact brief skeleton so formatting doesn't eat my time.

Assistant: Try a citation manager and a one-page brief template that highlights the issues, key facts, concise argument bullets, and the relief requested; which citation style do you prefer — Bluebook or a local court style?
Side_Note: [Tried courtroom sketching during an open hearing but felt self-conscious and disliked the distraction the activity brought to observation sessions.] 08/02/2025
User: I tried courtroom sketching during an open hearing and it distracted me — I need a less conspicuous way to observe that still captures useful details.

Assistant: Try replacing sketching with a short audio-note template and a concise observation checklist that records posture, pacing, and key rulings; would you like the checklist organized by 'judge, counsel, witness'?

User: I tried running a one-day ADR workshop, but the logistics were overwhelming, so I'd prefer lower-effort supports like short recorded modules or partnering with an organization to handle coordination.
Assistant: I can create short recorded modules and a partner outreach email template to hand off the logistics—would you like me to draft a two-module outline you could record?
